mechanism_narrative: A persistently low or falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive when policy is still easing or pinned near zero.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not above baseline after low FEDFUNDS observations, or if the signal's hit rate falls below 55% in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE identifies a worsening labor market and rising recession risk, which depresses equity earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than negative ones, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not significantly below unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in RSAFS signals stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which lifts near-term earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if higher RSAFS growth does not precede higher 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the slope of the signal turns negative in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while HOUST is also rising, real activity is broad-based and cyclical momentum is improving, which should lead to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint state INDPRO up and HOUST up does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 5-day forward returns are not positive in more than half of matched cases.

mechanism_narrative: A disinflation impulse, measured by CPIAUCSL slowing while FEDFUNDS stays near the lower bound, raises real policy accommodation and supports equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation triggers do not precede positive 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is unchanged near zero.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in HOUST captures weakening housing demand and tighter credit transmission, which leads equity investors to mark down cyclical growth expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by non-negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the signal has no incremental effect after controlling for labor conditions.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS is rising while UNRATE is falling, payroll growth and labor absorption confirm expansionary momentum, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-and-unemployment state does not outperform unconditional returns over 60 days, or if the sign flips when payroll gains coincide with rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing contraction regime, falling INDPRO indicates inventory liquidation and weaker industrial demand, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next week whenever industrial production momentum is still below trend.
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO momentum does not coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in contraction periods, or if the hit rate is not below 45% in that regime.
